MTR Fare Increase Hits New Highs

By Alex Tan, Staff Reporter
Sep 12, 2025, 2:30 PM EDT

Hong Kong's MTR Corporation announced its third fare increase in five years this week, drawing sharp criticism from commuter advocacy groups and lawmakers.

The new fares, which take effect December 1st, raise the cost of a typical commute from Tseung Kwan O to Central by HK$1.20. For monthly-pass holders, the impact is muted but still measurable — average passes will rise 4.3%.

MTR officials defended the increase as necessary to fund signaling-system upgrades and the long-promised North Link extension, both of which have been delayed by global supply-chain pressure on rail components.

Critics noted that the company posted a HK$10.2 billion profit in the most recent fiscal year, the highest since the post-2003 corporate restructuring.
